Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Looking at the image, I see the target is at the bottom right, and there's a white path leading to it. Maybe I need to find a chamber that connects to the target.
Let me try RIGHT from [8,32] to [8,38] and then explore from there. I know [8,38] RIGHT → [14,26] and [8,38] DOWN → [14,26]. From [14,26], I know RIGHT → [20,26] and DOWN → [20,14]. But I also know that [14,14] RIGHT → [20,14]. So [14,14] is connected to [20,14].

From [20,26], I know LEFT → [20,20] and UP → [14,26]. From [20,20], I know LEFT → [20,14] and RIGHT → [20,26]. From [20,14], I know DOWN → [20,20] and LEFT → [20,20].
So the explored chambers form a loop. I need to find a way to break out of this loop and reach the target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
def find_player(seg):
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24 and n['children']:
            return n
    return None

p = find_player(current_frame.segmentation)
print(f"Player: {p['boundary'][0] if p else '?'}")

# Player at [8,32]. DOWN didn't work. Let me try RIGHT to [8,38], then DOWN to [14,26], then LEFT to see if it leads to [14,14]
action(['RIGHT', 'DOWN', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
